name: Fried Green Tomatoes and Pasta
recipeIngredient: Cream, eggs, and milk or a similar dairy mixture
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the green tomatoes to about 1/2 cm thickness. Adjust as needed for frying.
2. Dip the tomato slices in cream, then coat with an equal mix of bread crumbs and grated cheese (about 50% each).
3. Heat enough olive oil in a large skillet or pan to reach medium heat but avoid smoking. Sauté garlic until lightly browned. If overcooked or at too high a temperature, it will taste unpleasant. Remove the garlic from the oil.
4. Fry the tomato slices until they form a crust and turn light golden on both sides. The tomatoes should be softened without becoming mushy. You may need to make additional batches of garlic-infused oil as you fry more slices.
5. Combine the cooked pasta with the remaining garlic-infused oil (ensure there is enough). Add any leftover cream, if desired, in small amounts. Top with fried tomato slices and a generous amount of shredded cheese and breadcrumbs.
Author's Notes: This recipe was created by my uncle, Larry Ross, who was an advertising director for Food & Wine and author of Nanny's Texas Table cookbook. It is intended to be a flavorful combination of crispy green tomatoes and pasta tossed in garlic oil. For best results, use fresh peppercorns and sparingly add Tabasco sauce when tossing with the garlic-infused oil.
